title: Ecktoria — Marble & Fire
label: chap:ecktoria

overview.tagline: The flame remembers. The marble cracks. The law forgets — but the statues watch.
overview.genre: Imperial decay, bureaucratic horror, memory as weight, Renaissance decadence — a city walled off from its own rot, where the wealthy dance while the poor cough ash.
overview.mood: The Flame That Remembers — And The Dancers Who Forgot
overview.starting_location: The Coin-House floor, marble cracked with soot lines, where votive candles gutter beside melted icons. A factor with smoke-stained fingers offers a folded note. 'The Lame King moves tonight. The Basilica burns with him. The Amber Palace hasn't answered a summons in a month. Find out who lit the fire — or the Vigil will audit your soul at dawn. And stay away from the Arena. The statues have been **whispering**.'

lore.history: Once the heart of the Utaran Empire, Ecktoria has worn its laurels into chains. Three centuries of sack, fire, and slow decline have not stripped its marble forums nor its gold-sealed coin-houses — but each day, more of its weight slips into the hands of mercenaries and whispers. The Black Banners march on its purse, avenging old Ykrul wounds by proxy. Processions still crown its stairways, yet ash lines every step. The city burns not only with memory, but with a grief that refuses to name itself. Yet the true sickness is not in the streets. It is in the **Amber Palace**, where the old patrician families have walled themselves away. They sit on laurels preserved from the empire, drinking wine that costs a village's harvest, whispering deals with merchants who have never seen a loom. **Maelstraeus**, the demon of commerce, has threaded their accounts with invisible tolls; every contract they sign forgets a clause. **Livaea**, the demon of desire, has wrapped their hearts in silk; they marry for influence, divorce for scandal, and love only the reflection of themselves in gilded mirrors — reflections that no longer blink. The common folk call them the **Unburdened** — not because they carry no weight, but because they have forgotten what weight **feels** like. The **Vigil** — once the empire's secret police, now a fractured ghost of an institution — fights a silent war over the dream of restoration. One faction, the **Censer-Knights**, serves the Everflame and the Synod, hunting heresy and latent magic with brass censers and bells that ring at dawn. Another, the **Unburnt**, answers only to the old patrician houses, ensuring that no whisper of rebellion reaches the Amber Palace. A third, the **Red Vigil**, has sold itself to Maelstraeus, auditing not coin but **memory** — they collect the names of those who have forgotten what they owe. They do not fight each other openly. They meet in the hypogeum of the Arena, under the gaze of statues that weep marble tears. The statues are not decorative. They are the city's **witnesses**.
lore.first_notice: The smell of old incense and fresh bread — and beneath it, the faint sweetness of rot. The way crowds part for a procession — and close again for a knife. The marble steps, worn smooth by centuries of petitioners. The way the wealthy never step on the cracks. The cracks are where the petitions of the poor are wedged. The cracks are where the Hollow reads.
lore.rule_that_kills: Never refuse a toast in a coin-house. The tallies are the true judges, and a declined cup is a declined trust. You will not borrow again. But more than that: never accept a gift from a patrician without naming its price aloud. Maelstraeus and Livaea both listen, and a gift accepted in silence is a contract written in water — it will drown you eventually.

entanglements.west: Kahfagia — Lantern-law fleets shadow the grain ships; tariffs are a polite fiction. The Amber Palace and the Beacon Crown exchange spies, not ambassadors.
entanglements.east: Acasia — The Broken Marches are a source of cheap mercenaries and cheaper grain. The Vigil has eyes in every free company camp.
entanglements.north: The Violet Steppes. Fortified towns and cities ward against Ykrul raids. An uneasy peace stands between the Imperial remnant and the native Vilikari.
entanglements.northeast: Aeler — Mountain passes choke with dwarvish tariffs; the True Masons hold the keys to the high roads. The Senate pays in iron, and the mountain remembers.
entanglements.south: The Amaranthine Sea — The old imperial navy rots in harbor; Zakov pirates and Thepyrgos chain-towers contest the straits. The flame of the Everflame does not burn on salt water.

quotes.speaker: Grand Magistrate (Elite)
quotes.text: The law is written in marble and witnessed by the flame. Every judgment echoes in the stones — but the stones have grown quiet. The patricians do not listen. They have forgotten what the flame tastes like. They seal their windows with silk and call it governance.

quotes.speaker: Ferrymaster (Commoner)
quotes.text: I've seen the city burn three times. The marble remembers the heat. The flame remembers the lies. The rich have stopped looking at the flames. They only look at each other — and at the cracks in their own mirrors. The fire isn't burning anymore, friend. It's **smiling**.

everflame_faith.description: The state religion of Ecktoria is a syncretic faith that venerates the Eternal Flame as the divine principle, but its power flows through three great Orders, each devoted to a different Patron. The Basilica of the Unquenched Flame is the heart of the city: a cavernous space of gold mosaics, weeping saints, and the perpetual smell of beeswax and myrrh. Mass is held at dawn and dusk, the congregation coughing through the ash that falls from the censers. The priests sell indulgences in the side chapels — a silver coin for the remission of a small sin, a gold coin for a large one, a horse for a murder. The choir sings in Utaran, a language most parishioners no longer understand, but the melody is beautiful enough to make them weep — and to make the statues turn their heads.

orders.name: Order of the Oath of Flame & Light
orders.description: The public face of the church. They lead processions, bless marriages, and anoint the dying. Their rites are those of vows, radiance, and protection. They are the ones who light the great candles at dawn. Many are sincere; most are tired. The tired ones are the ones the Vigil watches.

orders.name: Order of the Inquisitor Prime
orders.description: The **Censer-Knights**. They hunt untutored magic, root out demonic cults, and enforce the purity of the flame. Their rites are those of judgment, purification, and burning censure. They carry brass censers that smoke with bitter herbs, and bells that ring once for a warning, thrice for a raid. They are feared, but also respected — for they are the only ones who still take the flame **seriously**. And the flame takes them seriously in return.

orders.name: Order of the Oath of Mercy and Grace
orders.description: The healers and confessors. They run hospices, tend to the sick, and hear the confessions of the dying. Their rites are those of healing, forgiveness, and the spiral of care. They are often at odds with the Inquisitor Prime — mercy can look like leniency. The common folk love them; the patricians ignore them. The Vigil watches both.

everflame_faith.flame_power: The Everflame is both beautiful and ugly. Its gold leaf covers the same marble where petitioners once scratched their names. Its hymns are sung by choirboys whose families sold them for a loaf of bread. Its relics — a nail from the True Cross, a tear of Saint Lucia — draw pilgrims from across the empire, but the priests charge admission, and the poor sleep on the steps. The flame itself is a wonder: a pillar of blue-white fire that never gutters, tended by the High Priest alone. It is said to show the truth to any who stare into it long enough — and to show the same truth to the Vigil, who watch from the shadows. The truth is always the same: **you have forgotten something**.

faction_spectrum.name: Old Blood
faction_spectrum.description: The ancient senatorial families who remember when Ecktoria ruled the world. They worship precedent, hoard marble, and see mercenaries as necessary filth. Their power is in law, lineage, and the silent networks of the Censor's Hall.
faction_spectrum.patron_whispers: Livaea — they marry for influence, betray for pleasure, and mistake cruelty for wit. They mistake silence for consent.

faction_spectrum.name: Black Purse
faction_spectrum.description: The new power: condotta brokers, coin-house factors, and mercenary captains who have bought what birth could not give. They care about supply lines, not bloodlines. Their loyalty is to the next contract, and their god is the tally — but the tally has no god.
faction_spectrum.patron_whispers: Maelstraeus — they measure profit in margins, margins in suffering, and suffering in silver. They do not measure the weight of marble.

faction_spectrum.name: The Everflame
faction_spectrum.description: The priests, inquisitors, and relic-keepers who claim that the Eternal Flame is the only true sovereign. They offer legitimacy to whoever pays the tithe — and withdraw it from whoever forgets. Their power is in pulpits, processions, and the fear of excommunication.
faction_spectrum.patron_whispers: Neither — the Everflame remembers, but the Vigil has learned to filter its truths. The flame's truth is ash; the Vigil's truth is silence.

the_vigil.description: The Vigil is not one order but three, each claiming the mantle of the old imperial secret police. They do not cooperate. They do not war. They **audit** each other. They also audit the dead.

factions.name: The Censer-Knights
factions.description: Hunters of untutored magic. They carry brass censers that smoke with bitter herbs and bells that ring once for a warning, thrice for a raid. They answer to the Synod and the High Priest, but their true loyalty is to the **precedent** — the law that magic must be licensed, indexed, and taxed. A Censer-Knight raid is heralded by a single, low bell. They are the most feared and the most public. They are also the most forgetful.

factions.name: The Unburnt
factions.description: Bodyguards and spies of the Amber Palace. They wear red sashes and carry no visible weapons. Their job is to ensure that no whisper of rebellion, no bread riot, no unpaid toll ever reaches a patrician's ear. They are experts at **redaction** — not killing witnesses, but making them forget. They have forgotten their own names.

factions.name: The Red Vigil
factions.description: Collectors and enforcers. They serve no noble, no church, no magistrate — only the record. They audit the wealthy for the wealthy, finding hidden obligations, unpaid tithes, and compound tolls that have grown teeth. A Red Vigil agent never demands coin. They demand **names**. They also demand silence — the silence of the forgotten.

the_vigil.meeting: The Vigil's three commanders meet once a month in the hypogeum of the Arena, under the gaze of statues that weep marble tears. No record is kept of these meetings. No one admits they happen. The common folk say that if you see a Vigil agent, it is already too late — but they cannot agree on **which** Vigil. The statues know. The statues do not tell.

npcs.name: Valerius
npcs.motivation: Preserve the forms of law while the empire crumbles
npcs.quirk: His gavel is a sundial; it only strikes at noon. He has not struck it in a year.

npcs.name: Flavia
npcs.motivation: Balance the factions and keep the flame lit
npcs.quirk: She has never raised her voice; the flame speaks for her. Lately, it speaks in the voices of the dead.

npcs.name: Cassia
npcs.quirk: She rules from behind a silk screen; a dagger-hole is cut at eye level. The screen was a gift from Livaea. The silk is warm.

npcs.name: Jackson
npcs.quirk: He seals deals with three ribbons: white (ask), red (now), black (never). His black ribbon is fraying. He does not know who is fraying it.

npcs.name: Lucius
npcs.motivation: Own the next champion and the secrets of the hypogeum
npcs.quirk: His fighters' brands are his signature; he never repeats a mark. One brand is a spiral — the mark of the Red Vigil. The fighter does not know.

npcs.name: The Red Clerk
npcs.quirk: His inkwell is a skull; the skull belonged to a name he erased. He has forgotten his own name. The skull remembers.

npcs.name: Censer-Knight Commander Vorenus
npcs.role: Prefect of the Order of the Inquisitor Prime
npcs.motivation: Hunt untutored magic before it wakes the Ninth
npcs.quirk: His lantern burns green. He says it shows the truth. His shadow has two heads — one for each faction he has betrayed. He does not notice the third.

npcs.name: Lady Aelia
npcs.motivation: Outshine every rival before the next masque
npcs.quirk: She has not left the Amber Palace in seven years. She does not know the price of bread. She dreams of bread.

places.title: Petition Steps
places.subtitle: [LAW] [MEMORY]
places.description: Milk-stone worn smooth by a thousand hopeful knees. The petitions are written in ink and wax.
places.flavor: The steps answer in silence, and the silence is a verdict: 'Your claim is under review.' A clerk sweeps the steps at dawn. He never reads the petitions. He weighs them. The heaviest goes to the bottom of the pile. The pile is the city's memory.

places.title: Tally-Ford Ferryhouse
places.subtitle: [COMMERCE] [SECRET]
places.description: Rope-drums thrum all day; the ferrymaster watches coin and banners.
places.flavor: The rope-drums have a pattern. It spells the names of those who paid late — and the names of those who forgot they owed. The ferrymaster has a brother in the Red Vigil. They do not speak.

places.title: Everflame Cloister
places.subtitle: [RELIGION] [WHISPER]
places.description: Candle markets and side-chapels. The flame has a voice.
places.flavor: It is the voice of the last liar who tested it. Lately, it speaks in a whisper. The whisper is always the same: 'I remember. I remember. I remember.' Those who listen too long forget their own names and start reciting the names of the city's forgotten dead.

places.title: Triumph Stairs
places.subtitle: [POWER] [MEMORY]
places.description: Marble worn smooth by ambition. The stairs have a name: 'The Rise of the Forgotten.'
places.flavor: No one remembers why. The patricians still climb them at noon, their retainers counting the steps aloud. They never count to nine. The ninth step is a gap. The gap is where a senator fell — or was pushed — or simply forgot to step. His ghost is still climbing. He has been climbing for a century.

places.title: Censor's Hall
places.subtitle: [LAW] [SECRET]
places.description: Red-lacquered tablets record every name, every shame. The tablets are redacted in invisible ink.
places.flavor: The truth is underneath. The Vigil has the reagent. The reagent is made from the tears of the last censor who told the truth. He is still crying.

people_and_factions.title: Torchbearer Child
people_and_factions.subtitle: [SURVIVAL] [KNOWLEDGE]
people_and_factions.description: Ash-smudged hands, knowing every shortcut through the ruins.
people_and_factions.flavor: The ruins are not old. They are last year's fashionable quarter, abandoned when the mortgage was 'reclassified.' She has never been lost. The ruins speak to her in the language of eviction notices. She reads them aloud to the rats. The rats pay her in stolen bread.

people_and_factions.title: Decumanus-Master
people_and_factions.subtitle: [SURVEY] [SECRET]
people_and_factions.description: Surveyor who 'moves' stones. His rod is a finger bone.
people_and_factions.flavor: The bone belonged to the last surveyor who argued. He does not rewrite the map. He rewrites the **memory** of the map. 'This street was always a cul-de-sac. You simply forgot.' The Vigil pays him in forgotten names.

people_and_factions.title: Censer-Knight Commander Vorenus
people_and_factions.subtitle: [AUTHORITY] [TRAITOR]
people_and_factions.description: His lantern burns green. He says it shows the truth.
people_and_factions.flavor: His shadow has two heads — one for each faction he has betrayed. He does not see the third head. The third head is his own, from a timeline where he kept his oath. It is weeping.

complications.title: Blocked Bridge
complications.subtitle: [DEATH] [TOLL]
complications.description: A funeral procession for a pauper who died in debt.
complications.flavor: The law says the corpse cannot cross until the toll is paid. The family has no coin. The law does not accept stories. The corpse is beginning to smell, and the Hollow is beginning to notice a weight that cannot be buried. The Vigil is taking notes on who stops to help.

complications.title: Border Dispute
complications.subtitle: [LAW] [MEMORY]
complications.description: A surveyor has moved a boundary stone three feet east.
complications.flavor: The new map shows your warehouse now stands on a public thoroughfare. The old map is in the archive. The archive wing collapsed last spring. The memory of the boundary now exists only in the cracked floor of the basilica, and the basilica is accepting sealed bids for 'reparative memory.' The Vigil has already bid.

rewards.title: Procession Permit
rewards.subtitle: [AUTHORITY] [RELIGION]
rewards.description: Move sacred flame through a crowded quarter; law walks with you.
rewards.flavor: The permit is a torch. Carry it, and the city will part — or burn. The Vigil will follow. The flame will flicker when you lie. The permit is signed by a clerk who died three years ago. His ghost still signs.

rewards.title: Golden Edict
rewards.subtitle: [LAW] [PRECEDENT]
rewards.description: An exception carved in marble; precedent travels like law.
rewards.flavor: The edict is a crack. The crack in the law is now an opening. The Vigil is already inside. They will leave a note. The note will say: 'We remember. Do you?'

quick_use_notes.draw_rules: Draw until you have all four suits: Spade = place, Heart = actor, Club = pressure, Diamond = leverage. Highest rank sets the main Timer (2–5 → 4, 6–10 → 6, J/Q/K → 8, A → 10).
quick_use_notes.diamonds: Diamonds are codified outcomes (papers/favor/precedence) that change position rather than call for a roll.
quick_use_notes.ace_rule: If any A appears, echo **marble and fire** motifs — statues that turn their heads, flames that burn blue, law that remembers every name — and also echo the **Vigil's presence**: a cough in the crowd, a seal on a document you did not stamp, a mirror that shows a different face. The different face is always older. It is always watching.

additional_features.imperial_ruins: Every district bears half-collapsed arcades, serving as markets, courts, or shelters for mercenary companies. The Vigil knows every hidden passage.
additional_features.competing_factions: Dukes, guilds, and priests stage processions that collide at bridges and squares, each claiming legitimacy. The Vigil collects bets on the outcome.
additional_features.monuments_as_weapons: Statues, obelisks, and colonnades are not only symbols — they are barricades, rallying points, and high ground when the city riots. The Vigil has a map of which statues can be toppled.
additional_features.everflame_orthodoxy: The basilicas preach unity, but each sermon is a coded signal for a political camp. The High Priest plays balance while cultivating his own following. The Vigil decodes every sermon.
additional_features.black_banner_contracts: Foreign mercenaries fill the gaps left by a shrinking citizen levy, their loyalty secured only by steady coin. The Vigil audits their pay.
additional_features.amber_palace_quarantine: The patricians have not left the palace in a decade. No one is certain if they are prisoners or patrons. The Vigil maintains the perimeter — and delivers the wine.

local_patrons.name: St. Marcellus of the Gilded Scale
local_patrons.title: The Justiciar
local_patrons.description: His order enforces unwritten laws; reading a sealed precedent binds the reader to enforce it. His shrine is the Censor's Hall. The Vigil keeps a copy of every precedent.

local_patrons.name: St. Valeria of the Serpent
local_patrons.title: The Shedding Saint
local_patrons.description: Pilgrims who confess at her chapel forget one sin — and one kindness. Her mosaic shows a snake eating its tail. The Vigil uses her chapel to erase memories.

local_patrons.name: St. Lucia of the Undying Flame
local_patrons.title: The Witness of Embers
local_patrons.description: Any oath sworn over her shrine's flame cannot be broken; the breaker sees their own face in every wave until they drown. The Vigil swears oaths there — and never breaks them.

local_patrons.name: The Reckoner
local_patrons.title: The Tally Keeper
local_patrons.description: A mystery cult of coin-house factors. They offer credit at the cost of a hidden toll that compounds in blood. The Red Vigil are their collectors.

local_patrons.name: The Ember Prefect
local_patrons.description: A schism that believes the flame should not be contained. They meet in the arena hypogeum, chanting the **Unspoken Ninth Citation**. The Grand Magistrate has outlawed them three times. The Vigil has infiltrated them twice.

local_patrons.name: The Velvet Mask
local_patrons.description: A secret society of patricians who worship pleasure as the only truth. Their rites are said to last nine days. No one has ever left a rite unchanged. The Vigil watches their parties — and reports to the Unburnt.

lore_echoes.title: The Bitter Root
lore_echoes.description: Daughters of the Cord run a hospice in the Shatterline Bazaar. They ask no payment — but they cut threads. A renegade Sister hides among the arena slaves, trying to untie her cord from a coin-house weight. The Vigil has her file.

lore_echoes.title: Malachai, the Chained Angel
lore_echoes.description: A silver coin that never spends circulates among the grain-prefects. Take it, and your store never runs dry — but the coin will reappear in the mouth of a starving child. The Red Vigil uses it to mark those who owe.

lore_echoes.title: The Gray Wanderer's Grimoire
lore_echoes.description: Ecktorian psions are called 'Flame-Readers.' They see the future in the Everflame's flicker. The High Priest employs three — and has never trusted a forecast that didn't smell of smoke. The Vigil employs their apprentices.

lore_echoes.title: The Threshold Compendium
lore_echoes.description: The Imperial Arena is a Class IV Poltergeist site — the collective rage of every gladiator who died for the crowd. Saikou Ira would ring a bell eight times and leave a wreath of laurel. The Vigil rings the bell nine times. The Arena answers.

lore_echoes.title: The Ninth
lore_echoes.description: In any Ecktorian court, the ninth citation of precedent voids all previous. The wise stop at eight and settle. Those who cite the ninth hear a faint laughter from the marble walls — and their cases are never heard again. The Vigil cites the ninth often. The laughter does not bother them.

superstitions.name: Bread to the Flame
superstitions.effect: Crumble a crust into the Everflame. Ignore the first **Bread Riots** penalty.

superstitions.name: Knot the Ribbon
superstitions.effect: Tie a single knot in a coin-house credit ribbon. Cancel **Frozen Purse** or take –1 die on your next trust roll (your choice).

superstitions.name: Name to the Marble
superstitions.effect: Whisper a dead debtor's name into a statue's ear. Gain +1 Effect when collecting from their heirs this scene, but mark **Obligation** to the Grand Magistrate.

superstitions.name: Spit on the Seal
superstitions.effect: Before signing any contract, spit on the wax seal. If it sizzles, the Vigil has a copy.

minor_complications: **Exposure:** Your actions draw unwanted attention from **city guards or rival factions**.
**Noise:** Sounds of your actions alert nearby **crowds or ceremonial processions**.
**Trace:** Evidence of your passage marks your route for **patrols or inquisitors**.
**Delay:** A brief but meaningful setback costs you **time or ceremonial opportunity**.
**Supply Strain:** Mark +1 segment on a relevant **resource timer**.
moderate_setbacks: **Alarm Raised:** **City authorities or noble house** becomes aware and begins responding.
**Position Lost:** You lose advantageous ground/cover/stealth due to **crowd movement or procession**.
**Foe Appears:** A **rival faction member or city official** arrives on scene.
**Gear Trouble:** A piece of equipment becomes **Compromised/Neglected**.
**Lock/Barrier:** A simple obstacle now requires a test to overcome.
serious_trouble: **Key Gear Breaks:** A crucial tool/weapon becomes temporarily unusable.
**Major Twist:** The situation fundamentally changes — **procession turns violent/boundary dispute erupts/patron turns**.
**Rail Tick:** Advance a relevant campaign/front timer by 1 segment.
major_turns: **Trap Springs:** A prepared danger activates with full effect.
**Authority Arrival:** **High Priest, Grand Magistrate, or Duchess-Regent** intervenes.
**Scene Shift:** The environment changes dramatically — **procession erupts/arena gate opens/monument collapses**.
**Patron Omen:** Divine/arcane forces take notice — **flame speaks/blessing lost/curse manifests**.
**Narrative Pivot:** The story takes an unexpected turn that reframes objectives.
region_specific: **Ecktoria (Marble & Fire):** Statues turn their heads, flames burn blue, ceremonial masks appear in windows.
**Ecktoria (Procession):** Crowds chant your name, banners block your path, trumpets announce your arrival.
**Ecktoria (Patronage):** Seals break in your hand, favors are revoked, noble houses compete for your allegiance.
**Ecktoria (The Vigil):** A seal appears on your documents that you did not stamp. A cough from the crowd sounds like your name. Your reflection in a polished shield has a red sash.

cross_region_hooks: **Aeler True Masons bid** to shore a failing aqueduct; accepting stabilizes **Aftershock** but grants them soft leverage in the ward. The Vigil audits the contract.
**Aelinnel Green-Market broker** offers 'truth-credit' on court gossip; pay later in favors or names. The Vigil pays in silence.
**Black Banner retainer** parade demands back pay on the Triumph Stairs; crowd sympathy tilts your next parley. The Vigil arrests the ringleader.
faction_entanglements: If the party holds any **Condotta** Diamonds, begin with **Bread Riots** at +1 segment (mercenary grain trains re-routed).
Entering with an **Underway Pass** (Aeler) grants +1 Position on **Aqueduct Walk** scenes but ticks **Ducal Conflict** once (guild jealousy). The Vigil notes your pass.
Carrying **Green Market** tags downgrades one **Censor's Hall** action but flags **Inquisitorial Visit** at the next temple. The Vigil compares tags.
trade_good_consequences: **Arena Steel** → street duels spike → start **Arena Spill** at 1. The Vigil collects the weapons.
**Relic Fragments** → basilica prestige swings → tick **Church Purge** or grant **Basilica Audience**. The Vigil inspects the relic.
**Survey Rights** → boundary re-plat → downgrade one **Border Dispute** **or** upgrade a rival's. The Vigil updates the map.

diplomatic_favor.earn: Resolving a public scene without bloodshed (Forum, Procession, Coin-House).
diplomatic_favor.spend: Reduce DV of a permit/audience by 1, or ignore **Blocked Bridge** for one crossing.
diplomatic_favor.interesting: Holding 2+ makes you 'interesting': start a **Red List [4]** that advances on snubbed patrons. The Vigil adds your name.

reputation_echoes.crowd_loved: First time per session you face a mob, set Position one step safer. The Vigil watches how the mob loves you.
reputation_echoes.coin_house_trusted: Ignore the first **Frozen Purse**; next time, it returns at +1 severity. The Vigil audits your credit.
reputation_echoes.clerically_vouched: Downgrade one **Inquisitorial Visit**; if abused, promote **Church Purge**. The Vigil has a copy of your voucher.

monuments_as_mechanics.processional_geometry: When acting on the **Triumph Stairs**, choose: +1 Effect **or** –1 Heat; on a miss, **Rerouted Triumph** advances. The Vigil stands at the top of the stairs, watching.
monuments_as_mechanics.aqueduct_leverage: Securing an aqueduct span grants a one-scene **high ground** tag in any linked ward; if collateral hits, tick **Aftershock**. The Vigil knows where the aqueduct leaks.
monuments_as_mechanics.forum_acoustics: Rally speeches at the **Grand Forum** grant an extra assist die if at least 20 witnesses; failure spawns **Bread Riots** at +1. The Vigil counts the witnesses.

sb_conversions.1_sb: **Crowd Drift**: shift the scene's terrain in favor of or against you (bridges clog, stairs clear).
sb_conversions.2_sb: **Seal Revoked**: void one paper (Permit/Chit/Indulgence); to avert, sacrifice **Diplomatic Favor**.
sb_conversions.3_plus_sb: **Patron Bargain**: accept a visible mark (ribbon, laurel brand) to halve consequences; start a **Patron Weight [6]**.

hidden_orders: **The Ash Senate** — a roll of 'extinguished' patrician lines still voting by proxy through funerary guilds. The Vigil has the key.
**Surveyors of the Ninth Mile** — decumanus-masters who can **move** boundaries with ritual math; a lost rod sets precedent across the city. The Vigil owns the rod.
**Lantern Tetrarchy** — four basilica beadles who decide which miracles are counted; the fifth lamp was broken in a purge. The Vigil lit the purge.
**The Red Marble Compact** — coin-house founders who tithe 1 in 100 coins to a secret indemnity fund used to flip coups. The Red Vigil administers the fund.
**Archivum Umbrae** — censor clerks maintaining a **shadow index** of names that must not stand trial (yet). The Vigil reads the index nightly.
**Peregrine Brotherhood** — ferrymasters trading in sealed confessions; blackmail that floats. The Vigil buys the confessions.
**The Laurel of Dust** — a gladiators' mutual that chooses which champions live long enough to hold office. The Vigil chooses the champions.
**Aqueduct Collegium Obscura** — engineers who keep a second set of sluice keys; droughts become elections. The Vigil has the third set.
**Order of the Broken Obelisk** — survey-cult that believes Mile-Zero is **wrong** and can re-found the city with one stone. The Vigil has the stone.
**The Funeral Republic** — undertakers running a parallel courier state through catacombs and wakes. The Vigil uses their couriers.
**The Ember Vault** — a sealed reliquary under the Arena; victors' sweat was bottled for augury. The Vigil broke the seal.
**Triumphal Maskers** — procession costumers who encode heretical theses into robe pleats and torch patterns. The Vigil decodes the theses — and sometimes agrees.

cults_and_rites.name: Children of the Phoenix
cults_and_rites.description: Rebirth by fire: ash-marks grant +1 die vs. fear in crowds; mark Fatigue if you hesitate near open flame.

cults_and_rites.name: Verdant Laurel
cults_and_rites.description: Blood to marble, sap to coin: once/session turn 1 Harm into 1 Coin-House Credit; start a **Green Weight [4]**.

cults_and_rites.name: Mask of the Thirteenth
cults_and_rites.description: Forbidden procession step: walk through a riot unmolested; next parley with clergy begins one step worse.

cults_and_rites.name: Brotherhood of the River Tongue
cults_and_rites.description: Truth ferried at night: ask the Dolmis one yes/no; a ferryman shows up later to collect a name.

cults_and_rites.name: Candlemakers of Ever
cults_and_rites.description: Flame remembers lies: blue flame points to falsehood; the liar dreams of you for three nights (Exposure +1).

cults_and_rites.name: Sable Standard
cults_and_rites.description: War saint cult: your banner ignores morale checks once; start a **Blood-Tithe [4]** owed to the saint's chapel.

cults_and_rites.name: The Ninth Bell
cults_and_rites.description: Hour that is not kept: skip a queue or sentence; next edict you need arrives late (Delay SB free once).

cults_and_rites.name: Guild of Red Accountants
cults_and_rites.description: Sin as tally: convert 2 SB into a **Magistrate's Postponement**; add **Karmic Weight [6]**.

cults_and_rites.name: The Marble Eaters
cults_and_rites.description: Mortification sect: bite stone, gain +1 Effect resisting crowd crush; take Harm 1 (Stress) if you boast.

cults_and_rites.name: Torch of Saint Vessa
cults_and_rites.description: Saint of necessary arson: sanctify a fire to purge a tag (Plague/Corruption); City Heat +2.

schisms: **Orthodox Basilica vs. Ember Reformers:** Is miracle **counted** by law or **kindled** by need?
**Laurel Theologians vs. Street Saints:** Does glory flow **down** from triumphs or **up** from bread-lines?
**Iconoclast Ward vs. Reliquary Keepers:** Is the flame purer without images?

schism_mechanics.heresy_index: [0–6]: ticks when players publicize a stance, defy clergy, or wield cult rites in view. At 3: inquisitorial audits become common; at 6: **Church Purge** (Q) auto-triggers once/session until lowered.
schism_mechanics.sanctuary_anathema: Locations acquire tags during arc scenes. **Sanctuary:** first harm in scene becomes Fatigue; **Anathema:** first social miss spawns 'Proscribed Sigil.'

trial_by_fire.assembly: Set the forum/stair as arena; state thesis in one sentence (players or NPCs).
trial_by_fire.witness: Three proofs (relic, miracle, precedent). Each proof: test Social/Arcana/Resolve vs. crowd DV; on success, reduce Index by 1 or gain **Basilica Audience**.
trial_by_fire.judgment: Roll Crowd Mood (1d6): 1–2 mobs, 3–4 silent acceptance, 5–6 laurel toss (gain **Laurel-Deed**). If Index ≥ 4, –1 to the roll.

ecktoria_marble_and_fire.starting_location: The Coin-House floor, marble cracked with soot lines, where votive candles burn beside melted icons.
ecktoria_marble_and_fire.quote: In Ecktoria, even faith must balance its ledgers, and the gods audit in ash.
ecktoria_marble_and_fire.regional_motifs: Coin-oaths, basilica relics, marble acoustics, augury smoke, Vigil seals.
whispers_to_offer: The Vigil's informer sits in the corner
complications: Smoke speaks truths; coins stick to skin; a reliquary runs hot; a seal appears on your documents that you did not stamp
anchors_at_risk: A Vigil informer who owes you a favor
ecktoria_marble_and_fire.escalation_beat: The Basilica tolls a note only one PC hears. The Vigil hears it too.
ecktoria_marble_and_fire.relief: Return the shard; pay a city-toll in blood or Boons; route the procession elsewhere; give the Vigil a name.

boss_seeds.name: The Ember Prefect
boss_seeds.vigil_twist: The Prefect was once a Vigil informer. The Vigil knows where he hides.

boss_seeds.name: The Surveyor-Primus
boss_seeds.vigil_twist: The Primus has a Red Vigil agent as a protégé.

boss_seeds.name: Phoenix of the Arena
boss_seeds.vigil_twist: The Phoenix is a Vigil experiment. The Unburnt want to contain it. The Red Vigil wants to sell tickets.

boss_seeds.name: The Velvet Mask
boss_seeds.vigil_twist: The Vigil's Unburnt faction attends every masque. They are not there to stop it.

forbidden_leverage.title: Shadow Index Leaf
forbidden_leverage.subtitle: [SECRET] [VIGIL]
forbidden_leverage.description: Remove one name from the Red List for a week; add **Countercoup** +1.

forbidden_leverage.title: Ember Writ
forbidden_leverage.subtitle: [RELIGION] [AUTHORITY]
forbidden_leverage.description: Sanction one cult rite as 'orthodox' for a scene.

forbidden_leverage.title: Surveyor's Ninth Rod
forbidden_leverage.subtitle: [SURVEY] [SECRET]
forbidden_leverage.description: Redraw one ward line unnoticed (until dawn).

forbidden_leverage.title: Vigil Seal (Red)
forbidden_leverage.subtitle: [AUTHORITY] [DEBT]
forbidden_leverage.description: For one scene, the Red Vigil treats you as a contractor. The price will be collected later.

cross_contamination: **Aeler True Masons:** backing an iconoclast audit grants shoring crews; bell-codes now gate temple entries. The Vigil has the bell-codes.
**Aelinnel Thorn Courts:** hawthorn chaplains host 'mirror sermons'; a basilica gains **Sanctuary** while a rival becomes **Anathema**. The Vigil watches both.
**Black Banners:** war-chaplains monetize indulgences; clearing **Payday** risks +1 **Heresy Index**. The Vigil takes a cut.

patronage_and_power.description: Ecktoria's decline is not collapse, but transformation. Wealth bleeds into patronage webs: a senator's indulgence, a censor's indulgence, a noble's purse. The city's great houses keep mercenary captains on retainer, while priests sell ritual processions as public theater and private favor. And everywhere, the Vigil watches — not to stop the rot, but to take notes. Yet beneath the decay, two demons whisper. **Maelstraeus** teaches that every contract is a weapon, every obligation a leash. **Livaea** teaches that every desire is a chain, every pleasure a payment. The patricians who once ruled the world now rule only the Amber Palace, and the Amber Palace is a gilded cage. They do not see the bread riots. They do not hear the Vigil's footsteps. They dance. They drink. They forget that the flame remembers everything.

gm_advice.rewards: Tie rewards to visible symbols (seals, ribbons, laurel crowns, Vigil marks) that can be stolen, forged, or voided.
gm_advice.safe_conducts: Let rival patrons issue conflicting 'safe-conducts,' forcing players to choose whose favor matters more.
gm_advice.arenas: Use processions, funerals, or trials as arenas for social contests, where patronage weights surface and shift allegiances.
gm_advice.vigil_timer: Introduce the **Vigil's Faction Timer [6]** to track which Vigil faction (Censer-Knights, Unburnt, Red Vigil) has the most leverage over the party. When it fills, that faction makes a demand.

final_gm_mantra: Patronage in Ecktoria is not mere background — it is the engine of politics, coin, and survival. And beneath it all, Maelstraeus and Livaea are writing a contract. The city is the collateral. The poor are the interest. The patricians are the signature — and they signed in blood long ago.
